title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Inside the Debacle That Led to the Closure of El Paso’s Airspace
items.description: The F.A.A., citing “a grave risk of fatalities” from a new technology being used on the Mexican border, got caught in a stalemate with the Pentagon, w...

items.title: How leaders are protecting culture while AI rewrites how work gets done
items.description: At Ingka Group, the largest IKEA retailer in 32 countries, leaders set out to adopt AI in a way that wouldn’t put their culture at risk.

items.title: Harris Sold Email List to D.N.C., Then Paid Off 2024 Debts
items.description: The national party, which is nearly $100 million behind its G.O.P. counterpart, bought the list for $6.5 million.

items.title: Sick Detainees Describe Poor Care at CoreCivic ICE Facilities
items.description: Problems at detention centers operated by CoreCivic extend far beyond recent measles outbreaks.

items.title: OpenAI’s Codex and Anthropic’s Claude spark coding revolution as developers say they’ve abandoned traditional programming
items.description: New releases from OpenAI and Anthropic sparked an existential crisis among coders, but many engineers say they stopped coding months ago.

items.title: How ICE Failed to Justify the Shooting of Julio Sosa-Celis in Minneapolis
items.description: The collapse of the Trump administration’s version of events in the case was only the most recent instance in which officials gave an account of a sho...

items.title: T.S.A. Workers Brace for Another Shutdown They Didn’t Cause
items.description: As Congress leaves town without funding their department, airport security officers wonder, “How many more times am I going to be able to do this?”

items.title: Brazil, the World’s Largest Catholic Nation, Embraces Polyamory
items.description: More people in a still largely conservative and religious nation are rejecting monogamy as they seek new definitions of romance, and of family.

items.title: Rubio Stresses Shared History to Europeans but Also Warns of ‘Civilizational Erasure’
items.description: In his speech at the Munich Security Conference, Secretary of State Marco Rubio voiced some far-right ideas, but his diplomatic tone came as a relief ...

items.title: Japan Releases Chinese Boat Captain After Detaining Him for 30 Hours
items.description: The captain’s arrest had raised fears of a broader diplomatic confrontation between Japan and China, which have been at odds for months.

items.title: Scotty James Is Edged Out by Yuto Totsuka of Japan in the Halfpipe.
items.description: Scotty James of Australia laid down a strong run, but was outdone by Japan’s Yuto Totsuka.

items.title: Live Updates: Rubio Emphasizes Shared History With Europe at Munich Security Conference
items.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio also echoed the Trump administration’s talking points on the threat of Western decline.

items.title: Marc Andreessen made a dire software prediction 15 years ago. Now it’s happening in a way nobody imagined
items.description: The billionaire venture capitalist explained why software would eat the world in 2011. He had no idea.

items.title: Trump says regime change in Iran that ousts Islamic clerics ‘would be the best thing that could happen’ as another carrier heads to Mideast
items.description: Trump said the USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier, is being sent from the Caribbean Sea to the Mideast.

items.title: Bank of America lifts Moynihan’s pay 17% to $41 million for 2025
items.description: A year ago, Moynihan’s compensation was increased 21% to $35 million after the bank was able to boost its earnings.
